  • Examples

Unaligned_Data_Access

This example demonstrates several ways of using the unaligned data access macros. The first function demonstrates the use of vec_unaligned_load() to load a word from an address of arbitrary alignment.

The next two functions compute the sum of an array of unaligned unsigned integers. The first function, sum_unaligned_ints() uses the simplistic approach of calling vec_unaligned_load() for each address in the array. The second, sum_unaligned_ints_fast(), improves performance by using the streaming unaligned loads interface. It uses the vec_init_unaligned_array() function to initialize a vec_UnalignedArray object and then uses the vec_unaligned_array_load_next() function to load words from the array in sequence.

This example does not demonstrate the use of more specialized unaligned load functions like vec_unaligned_load_4().

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <vec_ops.h>


/* The vec_unaligned_load() function allows a single word to be loaded
 * from an address of arbitrary alignment.
 */
uint64_t
load_one(void* p)
{
  return vec_unaligned_load(p);
}

/* Sum an unaligned array of unsigned integer values by loading each
 * individually.
 */
uint64_t
sum_unaligned_ints(const void* ptr, int num_ints)
{
  uint64_t sum = 0;

  for (int i = 0; i < num_ints; i++)
    sum += vec_unaligned_load(ptr + (i * sizeof(uint64_t)));

  return sum;
}

/* Sum an unaligned array of unsigned integer values using the faster
 * streaming load API.  This interface takes advantage of overlapping
 * data from consecutive unaligned loads; it will issue only one load
 * instruction per call to vec_unaligned_array_load_next().  However,
 * this interface will prefetch a few bytes past the end of the array,
 * so the user must be sure that the extra loads will not cause a
 * protection violation.
 */
uint64_t
sum_unaligned_ints_fast(const void* ptr, int num_ints)
{
  vec_UnalignedArray my_array;
  vec_init_unaligned_array(my_array, ptr);

  uint64_t sum = 0;
  for (int i = 0; i < num_ints; i++)
    sum += vec_unaligned_array_load_next(my_array);

  return sum;
}


int
main()
{
  /* This buffer is word-aligned. */
  char buf[1000];

  /* Initialize the buffer contents. */
  for (unsigned int i = 0; i < sizeof buf; i++)
    buf[i] = i;

  /* Load one unaligned word from inside buf. */
  printf("0x%llx\n", load_one(&buf[33]));

  /* Sum 10 words starting at buf + 17 (an unaligned address). */
  printf("%llu\n", sum_unaligned_ints(&buf[17], 10));
  
  /* Sum 10 words starting at buf + 17 (an unaligned address). */
  printf("%llu\n", sum_unaligned_ints_fast(&buf[17], 10));

  return 0;
}
